text.title: Cloud Cost Tagging Policy Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the finops-devops-cost-tagging methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Enforce a required tag set — env, owner, cost-center, application, terraform — on every resource that can be tagged.
rule.rationale: Five mandatory tags cover environment, accountability, billing, app grouping and provenance with zero overlap.

rule.statement: Normalise tag values: lowercase, kebab-case, no whitespace, no synonyms (production ≠ prod ≠ Prod).
rule.rationale: Cloud bills aggregate by exact string match; synonyms shatter the cost report into unusable shards.

rule.statement: Quarantine resources missing required tags within 24h via SCP / org policy denying further write actions until tags are applied.
rule.rationale: Tagging slippage compounds — only an enforcement gate stops the long tail.

rule.statement: Inherit tags from parent resources where the platform supports it (auto-scaling group → instance, Kubernetes namespace → cost label) instead of stamping each child manually.
rule.rationale: Manual stamping drifts; inheritance keeps lineage intact through autoscaling and re-creation.

rule.statement: Publish a chargeback-ready monthly export keyed on (env, cost-center, application) and reconcile against the finance ledger before issue-day.
rule.rationale: Without a reconciled export, FinOps loses credibility with finance and tagging discipline erodes.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
